# Transcrição: 06-Setting Yourself Apart_ Premium vs. Commodity Coaching

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, now that we know why they're going to choose you Pikachu, we need to keep your coaching special. And I don't just mean unique. I mean premium. You know, when you go to a store, you could buy a handbag for $25 and it'll hold stuff. You can also go to maybe a department store and you could spend quite a bit more on a designer handbag. They're different. Okay. A premium coaching relationship is different than a commodity coaching relationship. And in this lesson, I really want you to understand the difference between the two. So let's head over to the whiteboard. The main difference between a premium and commodity coaching relationship comes down to how reactive the coaching relationship is versus a planful and proactive relationship. There are so many people in the world who really could call themselves a coach and that's so true. There are a lot of certifications out in the world that will teach you that if all you have to do is ask some questions that open with who, what, where, when, how, and you let the client guess what they're going to be talking about at the next session and it's just a call. A lot of commodity coaching out there says, Hey, just pay me X dollars per session and I'll be there on the other end of the phone line and we'll call it a day. That's a commodity experience. Anybody could really do that. And even if you put a certification on your website or a special badge that says that you got some training that said that you could do that, from a client perspective, that's not a special experience. When we look at a premium coaching experience, it's going to be anticipating the needs of the client. It's going to be proactive. It's going to be curriculum led. So the client doesn't have to guess what's going to happen at the next session. And in fact, when they're hiring you, they can have more comfort in the decision because they know, Ah, my coach has a map. They have an idea of where I'm going to be going. And instead of it just being a call, it's a full experiential experience. So what could make coaching experiential?

## [03:00 - 06:00]

Well, maybe when somebody hires you as a premium coach, very soon after hiring you ding dong at the door, there's a journal that arrives or maybe some other special gift that arrives. Maybe you have a special portal that's been designed for their coaching sessions. Maybe you're providing notes that are specialized for their session. Maybe you have special worksheets that go along with your curriculum that help them to deepen the experience that you're working on together. Maybe there's even more that you're adding to the experience that's deepening the transformation together. You could be just a phone number that people call. You could be trained to answer the phone in a certain way, but that's customer service. We want to take things to a whole other place in being a premium coach. So remember, being reactive is being a commodity. That's what pretty much anybody could do. Choosing to be premium is choosing to be specialized. So another example is hard to be proactive if you don't know where you're headed. If you haven't chosen a dot, that's one reason to stay reactive, right? So to choose a dot allows you to be proactive. When you're curriculum led and curriculum based, again, that gives you competitive advantage, but it's what the client does in fact want because they have a destination in mind. It's also a level of professionalism. It's something that you can show up with. Overall, the difference here becomes a behavior. It becomes an attitude. It becomes a decision. You know, I'm encouraging you towards like this premium side of the board. I also want to encourage you never let perfect be the enemy of the good. Never let perfect be the enemy of the good. When I was first starting out, decided to become the unicorn coach. I didn't even know how to curl my hair if you can believe that. I didn't know how to curl my hair and I thought that that was an all important thing that all fancy pretty coaches had to do. And half the time, like I said, I was wearing gym clothes. It's not really about what you wear. It's more about what's your decision about who you're going to serve. What's your decision about the framework you're going to take people through? What's your decision about the level of skill you're going to bring to it? And what's your decision about getting better at it? A commodity coach tends to have a fixed mindset about who they are being and who they're becoming as a coach. They stop at the certification. They stop at a level of skill.

## [06:00 - 08:13]

They stop at, here's my phone number. Call me at this time. And that is worth a level of compensation that is typically a lot lower than a premium coach. Now final idea here is that some coaches say, well, hey, when I let my client decide what we're going to be talking about during the session, I'm letting the client own the agenda. And I really want to remind you that just like when you go shopping and you decide whether you're going to buy apples or bananas or anything else, the client is always in control of the agenda because the client can choose whether or not they enroll in your program in the first place. And the client can choose whether to continue in that program or not. And what's really cool about coaching is it is that dialogue like we talked about in a prior lesson.
